* fix(admin): fix infinite loading spinner when /me returns 401

auth-store starts with isLoading=true. When useCurrentUser's queryFn
threw on 401, neither setUser nor clearUser was called, so isLoading
stayed true forever and the full-screen spinner never went away.

Fix: catch the error in queryFn, call clearUser() to clear isLoading,
then re-throw so react-query still tracks the error state. throwOnError:false
ensures the error doesn't propagate to the error boundary.

- Ensure chmod 644 so Docker bind-mount is readable by container process

* fix(api): allow X-XSRF-Token header in CORS preflight

The admin panel sends X-XSRF-Token on all state-changing requests
(double-submit CSRF pattern). This header was missing from the CORS
allowHeader list, causing OPTIONS preflights to return 403 and the
browser to block the request entirely — surfacing as 'Network error'
on the login page.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* fix(api): add allowCredentials=true to CORS config to fix browser sign-in

The frontend uses credentials: 'include' (ky client) so the browser
requires Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true in CORS responses.
Without it, the browser blocks all credentialed requests from
panel.zyntapos.com → api.zyntapos.com, causing a TypeError that the
login page catches as "Network error. Check your connection."

Capture ChatGPT's own sentinel headers via webRequest, reuse for rung call

Instead of fetching/computing our own sentinel+proof tokens (which fail with
403 bot detection), we intercept the headers from the user's real ChatGPT
request using webRequest.onBeforeSendHeaders and reuse those exact tokens
for our single invisible rung analysis call.

Fix nav state reset caused by Supabase token refresh

Root cause: Supabase silently fires TOKEN_REFRESHED every ~1 hour in the
background. The onAuthStateChange handler was calling setSession() on every
event including TOKEN_REFRESHED, which triggered the session useEffect,
which ran fetchAllData(), which set isLoading=true, showing the loading
screen and unmounting the current page — resetting all nested navigation
state 3 levels deep (view → activeTab → blueprintSubTab).

Fix 1 — Stop re-fetching on TOKEN_REFRESHED:
- Skip TOKEN_REFRESHED events in onAuthStateChange (session/data are still
  valid; only the JWT has rotated)
- Add loadedUserIdRef to track which user's data is already loaded; if the
  same user ID is already loaded, skip fetchAllData entirely

Fix 2 — Persist all navigation state to sessionStorage:
- view (App.tsx): restored on mount, cleared on sign-out
- activeTab (CoachingPage): which coaching sub-section was active
- blueprintSubTab (CoachingPage): which blueprint panel was active
  (advisor / evaluator / library)

Combined effect: switching browser tabs, token refresh, or any incidental
remount will restore the user to exactly where they were.

commit.message:
fix: URL-safe passwords and idempotent init (DEC-0057)

Critical bug: RedisPassword used base64 encoding which produces /,
+, = characters. The Go streaming service parses Redis credentials
as part of a redis:// URL, so these break URL parsing and crash-loop
the streaming container.

Fix: RedisPassword now uses randomAlphaNumeric(32), AdminToken uses
randomHex(32). Both are URL-safe. PostgresPassword was already safe.

Also makes tavok init idempotent:
- Detects running Tavok containers (skips port checks, pull, start)
